Insights

Scores by 2M inspector, and where each inspector is being assigned — a randomness check for production VPs, kept clear of PM identity.
Inspector ScoringAverage score given, by inspector
ConsistencyScore spread
Assignment MapInspector × neighborhood — review counts, last 60 days
Few reviews Typical Heavily assigned An even spread across the row and column suggests random assignment; a hot column means one neighborhood is seeing one inspector disproportionately.

Vendor Report

Trade‑level contribution to deductions, damage excluded since it's rarely vendor‑specific — weighed against how often inspectors are actually inspecting that trade.
RankedVendor contribution to deductions
ScorecardVendor deduction index
#Trade / VendorDeduction pointsShare of all deductionsTargeting indexRating
Targeting index compares a trade's share of deduction points to its share of items actually inspected. Above 1.20 means the trade is contributing more deductions than its inspection volume alone would predict; below 0.80 means inspectors are flagging it less than its exposure would suggest.
